hints.text: Short plates — blurred they show a **blue tinge or a hint of green on the left**
**Grey Street View car w/ visible roof rack + side mirrors** on ALL car coverage
hints.uniq_note: Only Laos has a similar car among tropical countries; the Dominican Republic's has thick black bar-stripes and no visible mirrors.

shared_with.note: The only other tropical country with a similar car.
shared_with.diff: Spanish + ALTO → Guatemala; Lao script → Laos.

shared_with.note: Similar car but thick black stripes on the bars, mirrors not visible.

hints.text: **Pastel-painted stone houses w/ rusted sheet-metal roofs**; exposed white brick also common
Roads: solid white outer + single yellow middle — **significantly thinner lines than Mexico's**

shared_with.note: Same scheme in Mexico with thicker lines.
shared_with.diff: Thin lines + grey roof-rack car → Guatemala.

hints.text: Poles very commonly painted **pink, green, or both**
Coverage fairly sparse, especially in the north (map of covered roads)
**Topography**: middle = mountainous; south = completely flat farmland, more built-up; north = fairly flat w/ slight hills/rolling
**Oil-palm plantations** in two areas: southwest + just north of the central mountains

**Completely green poles** → southeast (+ one eastern road further north + a stretch near Melchor de Mencos, far NE); their white **pole plates: top two characters = the road you're on** (infographic)

**Car-bar meta**: top-right bar faces FORWARD in most of the country; in the southeast it faces BACKWARD (often w/ a blue rope on the bottom-right bar); south + northeast also backward but **missing the thin black cap** on the bottom-right bar
Phone numbers: **2 = Guatemala City only**; **6 = Guatemala Department only**; 7 = anywhere EXCEPT Guatemala Dept; 3/4/5 = anywhere

**Spotlights**: Lago de Atitlán = by far the biggest mountain lake, ringed by volcanoes; jungle dirt road north of Tikal; small white bollards w/ 2 black stripes → road 1 NE of Quetzaltenango; PET-11 south of Las Pozas = rolling + perfectly N-S; CA9 (Escuintla→coast) = divided concrete highway
**Tikal trekker**: several Mayan temples just west of Tikal town — the most prominent ruins trekker (others exist, smaller)
White rectangular **'electricista' stickers** on poles = unique to Guatemala City
